title: Assign urgency level to patients based on symptoms
description: The system shall assign an urgency level (1-5) to each patient based on their symptoms evaluated by a triage nurse using a standardized assessment.
rationale: This requirement ensures that the most critical cases are prioritized, allowing for efficient and effective treatment of patients in healthcare facilities.
business_value: By providing timely and accurate urgency levels, the system enhances the efficiency of patient care, reduces wait times, and improves overall patient outcomes.

source.description: As a triage nurse I need assign an urgency level (1-5) to each patient based on their symptoms So that the most critical cases are prioritized

acceptance_criteria: Given a patient with severe chest pain and shortness of breath
When the triage nurse evaluates symptoms
Then the assigned urgency level is 1
And the patient is marked as highest priority
preconditions: A patient is present in the triage area with identifiable symptoms
The triage nurse has access to the system for inputting patient information and assigning urgency levels
postconditions: The system displays the assigned urgency level for the patient
The patient's record indicates they are marked as highest priority
Medical staff are notified of the high priority status of the patient
functional_constraints: The urgency level must be assigned based on a standardized assessment by the triage nurse.
Symptoms input must be validated before assigning an urgency level.
security_requirements: Patient information must be handled with strict confidentiality and access controlled to authorized personnel only.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system must respond within 5 seconds for each patient evaluation.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time during operating hours.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must handle up to 100 patients concurrently without significant performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: The interface should clearly display urgency levels and provide intuitive controls for updating patient statuses.

edge_cases: Patient presents with symptoms that are not covered by the standardized assessment criteria.
Triage nurse fails to input any symptoms.
System malfunction causes an incorrect urgency level to be assigned.

interfaces.name: Triage Nurse Interface
interfaces.description: The triage nurse uses this interface to input patient symptoms and assign urgency levels.

interfaces.name: Patient Information Database
interfaces.description: Stores patient data, including symptoms and assigned urgency levels.

test_cases.description: Evaluate patient with severe chest pain and shortness of breath.
test_cases.expected_result: Urgency level is assigned as 1, patient marked as highest priority.
Urgency level is assigned as 5, patient marked as lowest priority.

test_cases.description: Evaluate patient with symptoms not covered by assessment criteria.
test_cases.expected_result: System prompts for additional information or assigns a default urgency level.

test_cases.description: Triage nurse fails to input any symptoms.
test_cases.expected_result: System alerts the nurse to enter valid symptoms before assigning an urgency level.

test_cases.description: System malfunction assigns incorrect urgency level.
test_cases.expected_result: System is reset, and nurse can manually correct the urgency level.
